 '''''Level 153''' is the '''154th''' level of the '''[[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]]'''.'' 
}}
----

----
«•   ''' ''GENERAL DESCRIPTION'' '''   •»
----
[[File:Level 153.png|400px|link=]]
 The most infamous image of '''Level 153'''. 
'''Level 153''', known colloquially as "'''Antique Workspace'''," resembles an indoor office building of indeterminate size, lacking any outdoor openings such as windows. The Level's architecture visually resembles the late 1980s to the mid-1990s, comprised of peeling dull-colored wallpaper, generally addorned by a variety of wall decor emergent from the same period, antiquated drop ceilings, evenly adorned with a myriad of yellowing ceiling lights, and worn, dusty carpeting. Evenly spread across '''Level 153''', as its name denotes, are diverse work site offices' standards. Such areas typically feature an office chair, a work desk, various office supplies, and antiquated computer terminals. Furthermore, despite the Level's computer terminals being in moderately good condition, they are known to be prone to file corruption; consequently, they are infrequently used, with colonies instead opting to import '''''[[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]]''''' terminals from external levels. The Level's original terminals connect to an incognito location for the mainframe. Given the notable lack of entities nor an outwardly dangerous environment, '''Level 153''' is generally regarded as safe to explore & wander. However, once supplies are deficient at a Level, colonies need importation from others to keep operating. Nevertheless, the heavy influx of supplies, the moderately large population of the Level, and its proximity to '''''[[Level 154]]''''' have instead assembled for '''Level 153''' to become one of the Backrooms' more continuous extant trade hubs, with many groups seeking to establish outposts within '''Level 153''' to carry advantage of such.
[[File:Level 153_2.jpg|250px|link=]]
 Another photo of '''Level 153'''. 
While electrical wiring is prevailing functional at the Level, it is notoriously inaccurate & unreliable, resulting in outages & circuit trips being all the more common throughout the Level; however, these are rarely Level-wide & only seem to affect determined sectors. Moreover, exposed wires are a suitable uniform sight across '''Level 153'''. Many are known to cause electrical fires when retained in improper environments. Luckily for '''Level 153''''s inhabitants, nevertheless, the Level has a myriad of fire extinguishers & functional fire sprinklers, driving fires hardly ever deadly. ==''''' »   The Library of Time_ '''''==
[[File:Level 153_3.jpg|300px|link=]]
 A Library of Time during a blackout. 
Wandering through '''Level 153''' may occasionally lead to elusive, large libraries furnished with bookshelves holding multiple chronologically ordered manuscripts and travelers' journals, dubbed a '''Library of Time.''' Akin to the name, the books detail periods ranging from the prehistoric past to the distant future, offering insight into events that have yet to happen or be documented. Wanderers once thought it could predict the future based on the events described within these books, given the detail they can go into. However, owing to some predictions later being proven inaccurate or entirely false, many have developed theories about the future rather than narrating events that have happened in completely alternate timelines, considering that, on occasion, they can also reference entirely obscure concepts or ideas. Furthermore, as aforementioned, the journals of wanderers all over the Backrooms have been observed to appear on the library's shelves, having no limit to whose they may display. Therefore, this property has led to '''The Library of Time''' being considered an admiringly valuable resource to the [[Backrooms Wiki|Database]], as it can supply critical information about treacherous Levels without having to investigate them or even provide insight into possible undiscovered ones.
==''''' »   The Break Rooms_ '''''==
[[File:BE4C6682-4F6B-439C-8A09-E56B6A1352DC.jpeg|250px|link=]]
 An unowned Break Room. 
Another location that can be seen intermittently located around '''Level 153''' is "The Break Room." As its name implies, the Break Room resembles a break room of an office, furnished in a similar style to the rest of the Level. The visual appearance, layout, & kind of furnishing seem to vary widely from instance, but they generally share wood-paneled walls & unusually dim lighting, rarely lacking any. As predicted, Break Rooms usually furnish helpful supplies for wanderers; functional vending machines, water coolers, coffee machines, and cabinets stocked with food are all ordinary elements within a Break Room. Moreover, the kinds of food found within the cabinets are usually heavily processed and originate from various parts of both '''''[[The Frontrooms]]''''' & '''''The Backrooms''''', although fresh fruit, vegetables, & many kinds of meat coexist within the Level. Vending machines or large freezers can dispense snacks and drinks of various brands. Contributing to its anomalous safety, food & drinks in the rooms seem to be seamlessly supplied once every month, making Break Rooms a popular hotspot to seek refuge or settle in. Despite this, Break Rooms are highly valued spots by the innumerable inhabitants of '''Level 153''', as they are the Level's only abundant source of non-imported food, producing conflicts over ownership of one frequent & the lookout for an unexplored one constant.
----
«•   ''' ''ANOMALIES & EVENTS'' '''   •»
----
'''Level 153''' exhibits a variety of anomalous properties, all having to do with distortions & fluctuations in space-time. ==''''' »   Temporal Fluctuations_ '''''== Frequent fluctuations in '''Level 153''''s space-time cause the Level's time to flow differently close to other Levels, rendering time-telling devices useless. While this phenomenon isn't exclusive to '''Level 153''', with many Levels having time move at a slightly inconsistent rate relative to others, this one distinguishes itself for being constantly fluctuating. TIme passage is usually about 0.5 to 1.5 times faster or slower than '''''[[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]''''' timeUsed as a baseline for comparisons between the flow of time in other areas., time has been recorded to have been moving up to 16 times faster or slower, in one case making weeks in '''''[[The Frontrooms]]''''' just several days in '''Level 153'''. One cannot perceive these oscillations naturally, making them only measurable with communication between '''Level 153''' other levels. ==''''' »   Time Stop_ '''''== The '''Time Stop''' is an event within '''Level 153''' in which time in the Level will plummet to a standstill, where the environment will be seemingly "frozen" and cannot be interacted with. While there does not seem to be a consistent pattern or sign to when Time Stops occur, time in the Level tends to be less and less fluctuant for around a week before it happens. During this event, time will noticeably slow, causing actions such as falling objects and movement to appear sluggish & in slow motion. This continuous slowing of time will continue for the next 30 minutes, although it will feel like several hours to those experiencing it. At the end of those 30 minutes, time would've come to a complete stop, reportedly indicated by the sound of a record scratch. This stopping of time would last for another 30 minutes relative to baseline time; however, one would instead perceive the restarting of time as if it had taken place immediately after its stopping. The reason is that one's brain signals will also appear to stop all motion during the event. ==''''' »   Distortions_ '''''== During periods of heavy temporal fluctuation, space-time distortions may occasionally appear, acting as transport rifts between two or more alternate locations within '''Level 153''' or even other Levels, as has been reported. These visually manifest as blurry patches floating in mid-air or circular distortions upon walls or flat surfaces, similar to a ''wormhole''. When something passes through one of these rifts, it will end up at the end of a corresponding distortion in another region of '''Level 153''', yet many reports have a chance to send one to a completely different Level, typically a heavily glitched one or even the '''''[[The Void]]'''''.
----
«•   ''' ''COLONIES & OUTPOSTS'' '''   •»
----
Several colonies and outposts have been established in '''Level 153''' ever since its discovery, both to study its perplexing properties and take advantage of its economic value. ==''''' »   [[Republic of Level 153]]_ '''''== [[Republic of Level 153|The Republic of '''Level 153''']] is a self-declared sovereign state founded in 1982 and so far is the largest colony in '''Level 153''', with an approximate population of 5000 members in their central base and 8000 members situated around the level. The group narrowly avoided destruction during '''''[[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]''''' by evacuating its population into '''''[[Level 154]]''''', although 1/4th of the group's population perished during the event. After the end of the event, the Republic began to flourish, taking advantage of the increasing amount of trade flowing into '''Level 153''' from '''''[[Level 154]]''''' & the overall rising popularity of their level. ==''''' »   The Backroom Colonists (Vintage Sect)_ '''''== Possessing around 100 members, this outpost belongs to '''''[[Backroom Colonists|The Backroom Colonists]]''''', a relatively large & well-known group thriving through countless Levels across the '''''Backrooms'''''. The colonists have since improved relations and are currently in good terms with various neighboring colonies. ==''''' »   Backrooms Trade Outpost #012_ '''''== An outpost belonging to the Backrooms Trade Group formed by around 10-20 members, created solely to enrich themselves from trading amongst other known colonies within '''Level 153'''. Recently got in a moderately large scandal due to many attempts to exploit the Backrooms Stock Market using the Level's time-distorting properties just for their own benefit. ==''''' »   Kauer Temporal Research Site_ '''''== An outpost set up by the Kauer Research Organization to study the functionality of the Levels' many time distortions & fluctuating behavior. Much of their work is kept secret, with their location and member count unknown.
----
«•   ''' ''DOCUMENTED ENTITIES'' '''   •»
----
For the time being, no discernible entities have been discovered to reside within '''Level 153'''. However, there have been a multitude of similar reports of changes being made invisibly to the surroundings during the Time Stop event, such as objects being moved to different spots and files on computers being partially or entirely altered. Pervasive rumors around the Level state this is the work of an unknown entity, referred to by many as "The Anomaly," becoming an elusive urban legend around '''Level 153'''
----
«•   ''' ''RECOVERED NOTES'' '''   •»
----
'''“'''

“Time being linear and unchanging is the standard assumption. This is why most people have trouble wrapping their heads around the theory of relativity. Time being different in some places sounds at first like something out of science fiction. Eventually, some comprehend as fact. Then come The Backrooms. Space-time here is screwed up, and each Level is slightly different in its screwy reality. However, '''Level 153''' is still exceptional. While differences between relative time on different Levels are usually small and fixed, except '''''[https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Glitched_Levels those glitchy messes]'''''. But '''Level 153''' is safe and relatively stable, yet it has temporal anomalies comparable to those glitch Levels. Yet still, despite the laws of this place being beyond our comprehension, humanity has managed to survive, even thrive here. I guess it shows how adaptable we are.

—Backrooms Database Researcher ███████████

Well, you know what they say, the 80s aren't old, they are ''timeless''. —Backrooms Database Researcher █████████████

Please stop with the puns. —Backrooms Database Researcher ███████████”

'''“'''

“You want to know about The Anomaly, huh? Well, it's kind of like an urban legend around here. A story your parents would tell you when you were growing up to get you to be good. According to the stories, there is an entity that makes time in the level screwy, even though it is otherwise stable. It's also what's supposedly moving around our crap when time stops. The stories say that The Anomaly is eternally trapped beyond time, and even though it can't directly hurt us, it messes with us out of spite. That's about the only thing the stories agree about with The Anomaly. I've heard around 50 different accounts of its age, gender, appearance, you get the idea. One thing I noticed though is that none of the stories say how the thing came into being anyway. As for me, I think that the whole thing is just a legend, although that's also what you '''''[[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]''''' guys used to think about '''''[[Level List|this place]]''''', so you never know.

—Robert Woods, '''Level 153''' resident.”

----
«•   ''' ''ENTRANCES & EXITS'' '''   •»
----
==''''' »   Entrances '''''== *There is a rare chance to no-clip into this level from '''''[[Level 8]]''''' instead of no-clipping into '''''[[Level 9]]'''''. *Long staircases with varying lengths in '''''[[Level 4]]''''' can lead wanderers here. *Did you know that certain doorways and stairwells on '''''[[Level 47]]''''' may lead here? *An Orange Line train from '''''[[Level 154]]''''' has a chance to end up here. *Running certain programs on computers around the Backrooms may send one here. ==''''' »   Exits '''''== *Going through an elevator has a chance to send one to '''''[[Level 3]]''''', '''''[[Level 5]]''''' or'''''[[Level 130]]'''''. *'''''[[Level 4]]''''' may be entered via sections that appear more modernized. *No-clipping through yellow wallpaper similar to '''''[[Level 0]]''''' has a chance to send one there or to one of its Sub-Levels. *Walking through a neon-colored doorframe into nothingness leads to '''''[[Level 154]]'''''. *Noclipping through a flickering dark blue wall sends one to '''''[[The Blue Space]]'''''.
